title: Another. (Charms.)
content:
Let the superstitious wife
    Near the child's heart lay a knife:
    Point be up, and haft be down
    (While she gossips in the town);
    This, 'mongst other mystic charms,
    Keeps the sleeping child from harms.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Herrick delivers a powerful performance in "Another. (Charms.)"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
